top_categories.workout_context: 152 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Jämtlandspilen's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 152 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Jämtlandspilen's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 152 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
152 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Jämtlandspilen's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
At Jämtlandspilen, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
5,000ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,000ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Jämtlandspilen. Train it until it feels boring.
5,000ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Jämtlandspilen, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.heat: August in Östersund, Sweden can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Jämtlandspilen. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 152 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 152 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Jämtlandspilen. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Jämtlandspilen requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Gravel Specific, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 152 miles of mixed terrain in Östersund, Sweden.
